name: EB-5
tagline: A direct path to a U.S. green card through business investment
description: The EB-5 Immigrant Investor Program allows foreign nationals to obtain U.S. lawful permanent residence (a green card) by making a qualifying investment in a U.S. commercial enterprise that creates or preserves at least 10 full-time jobs for U.S. workers. It is the only visa category that leads directly to permanent residency without requiring a U.S. employer or family member sponsor.

requirements: Minimum investment of $1,050,000 (or $800,000 in a Targeted Employment Area — rural or high-unemployment)
Investment must be in a new commercial enterprise established after November 29, 1990, or a troubled business
Investment must create or preserve at least 10 full-time jobs for qualifying U.S. workers
Investor must be actively engaged in managing the enterprise (or invest through a USCIS-designated Regional Center)
Source of investment funds must be lawfully obtained and fully documented
No prior criminal history or immigration violations that would bar admissibility
benefits: Leads directly to U.S. lawful permanent residence (green card) — no employer required
Spouse and unmarried children under 21 receive green cards as well
No language requirement, no education requirement, no job offer needed
Conditional green card issued initially; conditions removed after 2 years upon proof investment is sustained
Eligible to live and work anywhere in the United States
Path to U.S. citizenship after 5 years as a permanent resident

steps.title: Source of funds documentation
steps.description: Compile comprehensive documentation proving the lawful source of investment funds. This is the most document-intensive stage.

steps.title: File I-526E petition
steps.description: File Form I-526E (Immigrant Petition by Regional Center Investor) or I-526 (direct investment). USCIS adjudicates the petition.

steps.title: Immigrant visa or adjustment of status
steps.description: Once I-526 is approved, apply for an immigrant visa through a U.S. consulate abroad or adjust status if already in the U.S.

steps.title: Conditional green card
steps.description: Receive 2-year conditional permanent residence. Continue managing or sustaining the investment.

steps.title: Remove conditions (I-829)
steps.description: File Form I-829 to remove conditions, proving investment was sustained and jobs were created.

processing.timeline: Total process typically 3–6 years from petition to unconditional green card
processing.cost: Minimum investment: $800,000 (TEA) or $1,050,000. Government fees: ~$11,160. Administrative fees for Regional Center investments vary. Attorney fees additional.
documents: Proof of lawful source of funds (bank records, tax returns, business records going back 5+ years)
Birth certificates, passport copies for investor and family members

faqs.question: What is the difference between a Regional Center investment and a direct EB-5 investment?
faqs.answer: A Regional Center investment pools your capital with other investors into a USCIS-approved Regional Center project (typically real estate or infrastructure). Job creation is calculated indirectly. A direct investment requires you to invest in and manage your own business and create 10 direct employees on your payroll. Most investors choose the Regional Center route for its broader job counting methodology and more passive management role.

faqs.question: What qualifies as a Targeted Employment Area (TEA)?
faqs.answer: A TEA is either a rural area (outside a metropolitan statistical area) or an area with an unemployment rate at least 1.5 times the national average. Investing in a TEA reduces the minimum investment threshold from $1,050,000 to $800,000. TEA designation is determined by USCIS based on census data at the time of filing.

faqs.question: How do I prove my funds are from a lawful source?
faqs.answer: You must provide a paper trail for the origin of every dollar invested. This typically includes 5+ years of personal and business tax returns, bank statements, property sale records, business ownership documents, gift letters if funds were gifted, loan documents if funds were borrowed, and any other documentation showing how you accumulated the funds. This is the most complex part of the EB-5 process and requires meticulous preparation.

faqs.question: Can I invest in my own business for EB-5?
faqs.answer: Yes. Direct EB-5 investment in a new commercial enterprise you own and operate is permitted. The business must be established after November 29, 1990 (or be a troubled business), and you must create 10 full-time jobs for U.S. workers. This route gives you more control but requires direct management involvement.

faqs.question: What happens if the EB-5 project fails?
faqs.answer: If a Regional Center project fails or is abandoned, you may face immigration consequences. However, USCIS has become more accommodating in recent years, and there are legal strategies — including redeployment of funds into a new qualifying investment — that can preserve your immigration benefits. This is why choosing a reputable Regional Center with strong track record is critical. We advise clients carefully on project due diligence.

faqs.question: How long does the conditional green card period last?
faqs.answer: The conditional green card is valid for 2 years. Before it expires, you must file Form I-829 to remove the conditions, demonstrating that your investment was sustained, the commercial enterprise was maintained, and the required jobs were created or preserved. Upon approval, you receive a permanent (10-year renewable) green card.
